'''高田 熊野神社'''（たかだ くまのじんじゃ）は[[神奈川県]][[茅ヶ崎市]]高田にある[[神社]]。大岡越前守忠相の一族縁の[[神社]]。高田熊野神社は当時の[[相模国]][[高座郡]]高田村の[[地頭]]・[[大岡忠高]]（大岡忠相の実父）が[[万治]]元年（[[1658年]]）に[[紀伊国]][[牟婁郡]]の[[熊野本宮大社]]から[[相模国]][[高座郡]]高田村に分祀し、'''高田熊野神社'''を創建。
摂末社の日枝神社は[[享保]]5年（[[1720年]]）、[[大岡氏]]が[[享保]]5年（[[1720年]]）、[[近江国]][[滋賀郡]]坂本の[[日吉大社]]から勧請した。
== 交通 == *[[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]]「[[茅ヶ崎駅]]」北口より 「文教大学行き」バスに乗り「高田」下車 徒歩３分
== 祭事 == *[[例祭]]：[[9月]]第2[[日曜日]]
== 外部リンク == *「神奈川県神社マップ[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.com/mem/main/196.html 『高田熊野神社』]」
{{shinto-stub}} [[Category:神奈川県の神社|たかたくまのしんしゃ]] [[Category:熊野神社|たかたくまのしんしゃ]] [[Category:茅ヶ崎市|たかたくまのしんしゃ]]

'''熊野神社'''（くまのじんじゃ）は[[神奈川県]][[茅ヶ崎市]]高田にある[[神社]]。大岡越前守忠相の一族ゆかりの[[神社]]。
高田熊野神社は当時の[[相模国]][[高座郡]]高田村の[[地頭]]であった[[大岡忠高|大岡美濃守忠高]]（大岡忠相の実父）が[[万治]]元年（[[1658年]]）に[[紀伊国]][[牟婁郡]]の[[熊野本宮大社]]から高田村に分祀し、'''熊野神社'''を創建。
摂末社の日枝神社は[[享保]]5年（[[1720年]]）、[[大岡氏]]が[[享保]]5年（[[1720年]]）、[[近江国]][[滋賀郡]]坂本の[[日吉大社]]から[[勧請]]された。
== 交通 == * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]]：[[茅ヶ崎駅]]北口より、[[文教大学]]行きバスに乗り、高田で下車。徒歩3分。
== 祭事 == * 歳旦祭（年賀祭）：[[1月1日]] * [[浜降祭]]：[[7月14日]] * [[例祭]]：[[9月]]第2[[日曜日]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.com/mem/main/196.html 神奈川県神社マップ - 熊野神社] * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0&PHPSESSID=ff8abecd9e6b1bb6e0394ca1c38c0d2f 熊野神社] — [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/ 神奈川県神社庁]
{{shinto-stub}}

{{神社 |名称 = 熊野神社 |画像 = |所在地 = [[神奈川県]][[茅ヶ崎市]]高田1-10-44 | 緯度度 = |緯度分 = |緯度秒 = | 経度度 = |経度分 = |経度秒 = |祭神 = 熊野大神
櫛御気野命
天神七座地神五座 |神体 = |社格 = |創建 = [[万治]]元年（[[1658年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[9月]]第2[[日曜日]] |神事 = }} '''熊野神社'''（くまのじんじゃ）は[[神奈川県]][[茅ヶ崎市]]高田にある[[神社]]。[[大岡忠相|大岡越前守忠相]]の一族ゆかりの[[神社]]。 == 由緒 == 熊野神社は当時の[[相模国]][[高座郡]]高田村の[[地頭]]であった[[大岡忠高|大岡美濃守忠高]]（大岡忠相の実父）が[[万治]]元年（[[1658年]]）に[[紀伊国]][[牟婁郡]]の[[熊野本宮大社]]から高田村に分祀し、'''熊野神社'''を創建したといわれる。現在の社殿は[[昭和]]2年（[[1927年]]）に再建されたもの。 摂末社の日枝神社は[[享保]]5年（[[1720年]]）、[[大岡氏]]が[[近江国]][[滋賀郡]]坂本の[[日吉大社]]から[[勧請]]されたもので、古くは山王山と呼ばれる丘隆地に建てられていたが、のちに熊野神社境内に移された。 == 祭神 == * 熊野大神 * 櫛御気野命 * 天神七座地神五座 * 大山咋命（日枝神社祭神） == 交通 == * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]]：[[茅ヶ崎駅]]北口より、[[文教大学]]行きバスに乗り、高田で下車。徒歩3分。 == 祭事 == * 歳旦祭（年賀祭）：[[1月1日]] * [[浜降祭]]：[[7月14日]] * [[例祭]]：[[9月]]第2[[日曜日]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.com/mem/main/196.html 神奈川県神社マップ - 熊野神社] * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0&PHPSESSID=ff8abecd9e6b1bb6e0394ca1c38c0d2f 熊野神社] — [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/ 神奈川県神社庁] {{DEFAULTSORT:たかたくまのしんしや}} [[Category:神奈川県の神社]] [[Category:熊野神社|たかたくまのしんしや ちかさきし]] [[Category:茅ヶ崎市の歴史]] {{shinto-stub}}

天神七座地神五座 |神体 = |社格 = |創建 = [[万治]]元年（[[1658年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[9月]]第2[[日曜日]] |神事 = }} '''熊野神社'''（くまのじんじゃ）は[[神奈川県]][[茅ヶ崎市]]高田にある[[神社]]。[[大岡忠相|大岡越前守忠相]]の一族ゆかりの[[神社]]。 == 由緒 == （由緒節の主要な出典は神奈川県神社庁ウェブサイト{{Cite web |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0 |title=熊野神社 |publisher=神奈川県神社庁 |accessdate=2018-12-27 }}） 熊野神社は当時の[[相模国]][[高座郡]]高田村の[[地頭]]であった[[大岡忠高|大岡美濃守忠高]]（大岡忠相の実父）が[[万治]]元年（[[1658年]]）に[[紀伊国]][[牟婁郡]]の[[熊野本宮大社]]から高田村に分祀し、'''熊野神社'''を創建したといわれる。現在の社殿は[[昭和]]2年（[[1927年]]）に再建されたもの。 摂末社の日枝神社は[[享保]]5年（[[1720年]]）、[[大岡氏]]が[[近江国]][[滋賀郡]]坂本の[[日吉大社]]から[[勧請]]されたもので、古くは山王山と呼ばれる丘隆地に建てられていたが、のちに熊野神社境内に移された。 == 祭神 == * 熊野大神 * 櫛御気野命 * 天神七座地神五座 * 大山咋命（日枝神社祭神） == 交通 == * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]]：[[茅ヶ崎駅]]北口より、[[文教大学]]行きバスに乗り、高田で下車。徒歩3分。 == 祭事 == * 歳旦祭（年賀祭）：[[1月1日]] * [[浜降祭]]：[[7月14日]] * [[例祭]]：[[9月]]第2[[日曜日]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.com/mem/main/196.html 神奈川県神社マップ - 熊野神社] * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0&PHPSESSID=ff8abecd9e6b1bb6e0394ca1c38c0d2f 熊野神社] — [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/ 神奈川県神社庁] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかたくまのしんしや}} [[Category:神奈川県の神社]] [[Category:熊野神社|たかたくまのしんしや ちかさきし]] [[Category:茅ヶ崎市の歴史]] [[Category:茅ヶ崎市の建築物]]

天神七座地神五座 |神体 = |社格 = |創建 = [[万治]]元年（[[1658年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[9月]]第2[[日曜日]] |神事 = }} '''熊野神社'''（くまのじんじゃ）は[[神奈川県]][[茅ヶ崎市]]高田にある[[神社]]。[[大岡忠相|大岡越前守忠相]]の一族ゆかりの[[神社]]。 == 由緒 == （由緒節の主要な出典は神奈川県神社庁ウェブサイト{{Cite web |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0 |title=熊野神社 |publisher=神奈川県神社庁 |accessdate=2018-12-27 }}） 熊野神社は当時の[[相模国]][[高座郡]]高田村の[[地頭]]であった[[大岡忠高|大岡美濃守忠高]]（大岡忠相の実父）が[[万治]]元年（[[1658年]]）に[[紀伊国]][[牟婁郡]]の[[熊野本宮大社]]から高田村に分祀し、'''熊野神社'''を創建したといわれる。現在の社殿は[[昭和]]2年（[[1927年]]）に再建されたもの。 摂末社の日枝神社は[[享保]]5年（[[1720年]]）、[[大岡氏]]が[[近江国]][[滋賀郡]]坂本の[[日吉大社]]から[[勧請]]されたもので、古くは山王山と呼ばれる丘隆地に建てられていたが、のちに熊野神社境内に移された。 == 祭神 == * 熊野大神 * 櫛御気野命 * 天神七座地神五座 * 大山咋命（日枝神社祭神） == 境内社 == * [[日枝神社]] == 交通 == * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]]：[[茅ケ崎駅]]北口より、[[文教大学]]行きバスに乗り、高田で下車。徒歩3分。 == 祭事 == * 歳旦祭（年賀祭）：[[1月1日]] * [[浜降祭]]：[[7月14日]] * [[例祭]]：[[9月]]第2[[日曜日]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連文献 == * {{cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第38巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]3巻之61村里部高座郡巻之3|chapter=大庭庄 高田村 熊野社|id={{NDLJP|1179219/150}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.com/mem/main/196.html 神奈川県神社マップ - 熊野神社] * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0&PHPSESSID=ff8abecd9e6b1bb6e0394ca1c38c0d2f 熊野神社] — [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/ 神奈川県神社庁] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかたくまのしんしや}} [[Category:神奈川県の神社]] [[Category:熊野神社|たかたくまのしんしや ちかさきし]] [[Category:茅ヶ崎市の歴史]] [[Category:茅ヶ崎市の建築物]]

[[ja:%E9%AB%98%E7%94%B0%E7%86%8A%E9%87%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E8%8C%85%E3%83%B6%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]]

{{Shrine | Name = Kumano Shrine | Image = [[File:Kumono shrine chigasaki Kanagawa 2023 Jan 20 various 17 42 44 119000.jpeg|270px]]
Torii and Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|55.6|N|139|24|54.6|E}}}} | Location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}} Takata 1-10-44 | LatitudeDeg = 35 | LatitudeMin = 20 | LatitudeSec = 55.6 | NS = N | LongitudeDeg = 139 | LongitudeMin = 24 | LongitudeSec = 54.6 | EW = E | CountryCode = JP | Map = Japan Kanagawa#Japan | Deities = Kumano Ōkami
Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto
Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities | SacredObject = | Rank = | Founded = {{ill|Manji|ja|万治}} First Year (1658) | MainBuilding = | AlternateNames = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = {{ill|Second Sunday of September|ja|9月第2日曜日}} | Rituals = }} '''Kumano Shrine''' (熊野神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Takata, {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is historically linked to the family of {{ill|Ōoka Tadasuke|ja|大岡忠相}}, the famous magistrate of the Edo period. == History == (This section primarily references the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association website{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0 |title=Kumano Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2018-12-27 }}. Additional sources are noted where applicable.) Kumano Shrine was established in {{ill|Manji|ja|万治}} First Year (1658) by {{ill|Ōoka Tadataka|ja|大岡忠高}}, the headman of Takata Village in the {{ill|Kōza District|ja|高座郡}}, {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}} (father of Ōoka Tadasuke). The shrine enshrines a divided deity from the {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Muro District, Wakayama|ja|牟婁郡}}. The current shrine building was reconstructed in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 2 (1927). The subordinate shrine, {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)}}, was brought from {{ill|Hie Taisha|ja|日吉大社}} in {{ill|Sakamoto, Shiga|ja|坂本 (滋賀郡)}} in {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 5 (1720) by the Ōoka family. Originally located on a hill known as Mount Sannō, it was later moved to the grounds of Kumano Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Kumano Ōkami * Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto * Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities * Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto (Hie Shrine deity) == Subordinate Shrines == * {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)}} == Access == * **By car**: Located along Kumano Shrine Road. Parking available nearby. - Approximately 12 km south from {{ill|National Route 1 (Japan)|ja|国道1号}} Amagawa intersection. - Approximately 2 km north from {{ill|National Route 163 (Japan)|ja|国道163号}} Kitatawara Bridge intersection. * **By public transportation**: - From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅}} (North Exit), take a bus bound for {{ill|Bunkyo University|ja|文教大学}} and get off at Takata. Walk for 3 minutes. == Festivals == * **New Year's Festival**: {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}} * **Hamaori Festival**: {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日}} * **Annual Festival**: Second Sunday of {{ill|September|ja|9月}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{cite book|ja|title=Great Japan Geographical Chronicles|volume=Volume 38: New Edition of Sagami Province Topographical Records, Scroll 3|chapter=Ōba Manor, Takata Village, Kumano Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1179219/150}}|publisher=Yuzankaku|date=August 1932}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.com/mem/main/196.html Kanagawa Shrine Map - Kumano Shrine] * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0&PHPSESSID=ff8abecd9e6b1bb6e0394ca1c38c0d2f Kumano Shrine] — [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/ Kanagawa Shrine Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto template}} [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[Category:History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Buildings in Chigasaki]] [[ja:%E9%AB%98%E7%94%B0%E7%86%8A%E9%87%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E8%8C%85%E3%83%B6%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]]

Torii and Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|55.6|N|139|24|54.6|E}}}} | Location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}} Takata 1-10-44 | LatitudeDeg = 35 | LatitudeMin = 20 | LatitudeSec = 55.6 | NS = N | LongitudeDeg = 139 | LongitudeMin = 24 | LongitudeSec = 54.6 | EW = E | CountryCode = JP | Map = Japan Kanagawa#Japan | Deities = Kumano Ōkami
Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto
Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities | SacredObject = | Rank = | Founded = {{ill|Manji|ja|万治}} First Year (1658) | MainBuilding = | AlternateNames = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = {{ill|Second Sunday of September|ja|9月第2日曜日}} | Rituals = }} '''Kumano Shrine''' (熊野神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Takata, {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is historically linked to the family of {{ill|Ōoka Tadasuke|ja|大岡忠相}}, the famous magistrate of the Edo period. == History == (This section primarily references the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association website{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0 |title=Kumano Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2018-12-27 }}. Additional sources are noted where applicable.) Kumano Shrine was established in {{ill|Manji|ja|万治}} First Year (1658) by {{ill|Ōoka Tadataka|ja|大岡忠高}}, the headman of Takata Village in the {{ill|Kōza District|ja|高座郡}}, {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}} (father of Ōoka Tadasuke). The shrine enshrines a divided deity from the {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Muro District, Wakayama|ja|牟婁郡}}. The current shrine building was reconstructed in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 2 (1927). The subordinate shrine, {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)}}, was brought from {{ill|Hie Taisha|ja|日吉大社}} in {{ill|Sakamoto, Shiga|ja|坂本 (滋賀郡)}} in {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 5 (1720) by the Ōoka family. Originally located on a hill known as Mount Sannō, it was later moved to the grounds of Kumano Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Kumano Ōkami * Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto * Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities * Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto (Hie Shrine deity) == Subordinate Shrines == * {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)}} == Access == * **By car**: Located along Kumano Shrine Road. Parking available nearby. - Approximately 12 km south from {{ill|National Route 1 (Japan)|ja|国道1号}} Amagawa intersection. - Approximately 2 km north from {{ill|National Route 163 (Japan)|ja|国道163号}} Kitatawara Bridge intersection. * **By public transportation**: - From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅}} (North Exit), take a bus bound for {{ill|Bunkyo University|ja|文教大学}} and get off at Takata. Walk for 3 minutes. == Festivals == * **New Year's Festival**: {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}} * **Hamaori Festival**: {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日}} * **Annual Festival**: Second Sunday of {{ill|September|ja|9月}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{cite book|ja|title=Great Japan Geographical Chronicles|volume=Volume 38: New Edition of Sagami Province Topographical Records, Scroll 3|chapter=Ōba Manor, Takata Village, Kumano Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1179219/150}}|publisher=Yuzankaku|date=August 1932}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.com/mem/main/196.html Kanagawa Shrine Map - Kumano Shrine] * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0&PHPSESSID=ff8abecd9e6b1bb6e0394ca1c38c0d2f Kumano Shrine] — [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/ Kanagawa Shrine Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto template}} [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[Category:History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Buildings in Chigasaki]] [[ja:%E9%AB%98%E7%94%B0%E7%86%8A%E9%87%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E8%8C%85%E3%83%B6%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]]

{{Shrine | Name = Kumano Shrine | Image = [[File:Kumono shrine chigasaki Kanagawa 2023 Jan 20 various 17 42 44 119000.jpeg|270px]]
Torii and Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|55.6|N|139|24|54.6|E}}}} | Location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}} Takata 1-10-44 | LatitudeDeg = 35 | LatitudeMin = 20 | LatitudeSec = 55.6 | NS = N | LongitudeDeg = 139 | LongitudeMin = 24 | LongitudeSec = 54.6 | EW = E | CountryCode = JP | Map = Japan Kanagawa#Japan | Deities = Kumano Ōkami
Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto
Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities | SacredObject = | Rank = | Founded = {{ill|Manji|ja|万治}} First Year (1658) | MainBuilding = | AlternateNames = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = {{ill|Second Sunday of September|ja|9月第2日曜日}} | Rituals = }} '''Kumano Shrine''' (熊野神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Takata, {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is historically linked to the family of {{ill|Ōoka Tadasuke|ja|大岡忠相}}, the famous magistrate of the Edo period. == History == (This section primarily references the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association website{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0 |title=Kumano Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2018-12-27 }}. Additional sources are noted where applicable.) Kumano Shrine was established in {{ill|Manji|ja|万治}} First Year (1658) by {{ill|Ōoka Tadataka|ja|大岡忠高}}, the headman of Takata Village in the {{ill|Kōza District|ja|高座郡}}, {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}} (father of Ōoka Tadasuke). The shrine enshrines a divided deity from the {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Muro District, Wakayama|ja|牟婁郡}}. The current shrine building was reconstructed in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 2 (1927). The subordinate shrine, {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)}}, was brought from {{ill|Hie Taisha|ja|日吉大社}} in {{ill|Sakamoto, Shiga|ja|坂本 (滋賀郡)}} in {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 5 (1720) by the Ōoka family. Originally located on a hill known as Mount Sannō, it was later moved to the grounds of Kumano Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Kumano Ōkami * Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto * Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities * Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto (Hie Shrine deity) == Subordinate Shrines == * {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)}} == Access == * **By car**: Located along Kumano Shrine Road. Parking available nearby. - Approximately 12 km south from {{ill|National Route 1 (Japan)|ja|国道1号}} Amagawa intersection. - Approximately 2 km north from {{ill|National Route 163 (Japan)|ja|国道163号}} Kitatawara Bridge intersection. * **By public transportation**: - From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅}} (North Exit), take a bus bound for {{ill|Bunkyo University|ja|文教大学}} and get off at Takata. Walk for 3 minutes. == Festivals == * **New Year's Festival**: {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}} * **Hamaori Festival**: {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日}} * **Annual Festival**: Second Sunday of {{ill|September|ja|9月}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{cite book|ja|title=Great Japan Geographical Chronicles|volume=Volume 38: New Edition of Sagami Province Topographical Records, Scroll 3|chapter=Ōba Manor, Takata Village, Kumano Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1179219/150}}|publisher=Yuzankaku|date=August 1932}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.com/mem/main/196.html Kanagawa Shrine Map - Kumano Shrine] * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0&PHPSESSID=ff8abecd9e6b1bb6e0394ca1c38c0d2f Kumano Shrine] — [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/ Kanagawa Shrine Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto template}} [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[Category:History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Buildings in Chigasaki]] [[ja:%E9%AB%98%E7%94%B0%E7%86%8A%E9%87%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E8%8C%85%E3%83%B6%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:bump 1-25]]

Kumano Shrine was established in {{ill|Manji|ja|万治}} First Year (1658) by {{ill|Ōoka Tadataka|ja|大岡忠高}}, the headman of Takata Village in the {{ill|Kōza District|ja|高座郡}}, {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}} (father of Ōoka Tadasuke). Parking available nearby. - Approximately 12 km south from {{ill|National Route 1 (Japan)|ja|国道1号}} Amagawa intersection. - Approximately 2 km north from {{ill|National Route 163 (Japan)|ja|国道163号}} Kitatawara Bridge intersection. * **By public transportation**: - From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅}} (North Exit), take a bus bound for {{ill|Bunkyo University|ja|文教大学}} and get off at Takata. Walk for 3 minutes. == Festivals == * **New Year's Festival**: {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}} * **Hamaori Festival**: {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日}} * **festival**: Second Sunday of {{ill|September|ja|9月}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{cite book|ja|title=Great Japan Geographical Chronicles|volume=Volume 38: New Edition of Sagami Province Topographical Records, Scroll 3|chapter=Ōba Manor, Takata Village, Kumano Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1179219/150}}|publisher=Yuzankaku|date=August 1932}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.com/mem/main/196.html Kanagawa Shrine Map - Kumano Shrine] * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0&PHPSESSID=ff8abecd9e6b1bb6e0394ca1c38c0d2f Kumano Shrine] — [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/ Kanagawa Shrine Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto template}} [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[Category:History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Buildings in Chigasaki]] [[ja:%E9%AB%98%E7%94%B0%E7%86%8A%E9%87%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E8%8C%85%E3%83%B6%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:bump 1-25]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Kumano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kumono shrine chigasaki Kanagawa 2023 Jan 20 various 17 42 44 119000.jpeg|270px]]
Torii and Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|55.6|N|139|24|54.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}} Takata 1-10-44 | LatitudeDeg = 35 | LatitudeMin = 20 | LatitudeSec = 55.6 | NS = N | LongitudeDeg = 139 | LongitudeMin = 24 | LongitudeSec = 54.6 | EW = E | CountryCode = JP | Map = Japan Kanagawa#Japan | Deities = Kumano Ōkami
Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto
Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities | SacredObject = | Rank = | consecration_year = {{ill|Manji|ja|万治}} First Year (1658) | MainBuilding = | Alternatenames = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = {{ill|Second Sunday of September|ja|9月第2日曜日}} | Rituals = }} '''Kumano Shrine''' (熊野神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Takata, {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is historically linked to the family of {{ill|Ōoka Tadasuke|ja|大岡忠相}}, the famous magistrate of the Edo period. == History == (This section primarily references the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association website{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0 |title=Kumano Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2018-12-27 }}. Additional sources are noted where applicable.) Kumano Shrine was established in {{ill|Manji|ja|万治}} First Year (1658) by {{ill|Ōoka Tadataka|ja|大岡忠高}}, the headman of Takata Village in the {{ill|Kōza District|ja|高座郡}}, {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}} (father of Ōoka Tadasuke). The shrine enshrines a divided deity from the {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Muro District, Wakayama|ja|牟婁郡}}. The current shrine building was reconstructed in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 2 (1927). The subordinate shrine, {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)}}, was brought from {{ill|Hie Taisha|ja|日吉大社}} in {{ill|Sakamoto, Shiga|ja|坂本 (滋賀郡)}} in {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 5 (1720) by the Ōoka family. Originally located on a hill known as Mount Sannō, it was later moved to the grounds of Kumano Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Kumano Ōkami * Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto * Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities * Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto (Hie Shrine deity) == Subordinate Shrines == * {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)}} == Access == * **By car**: Located along Kumano Shrine Road. Parking available nearby. - Approximately 12 km south from {{ill|National Route 1 (Japan)|ja|国道1号}} Amagawa intersection. - Approximately 2 km north from {{ill|National Route 163 (Japan)|ja|国道163号}} Kitatawara Bridge intersection. * **By public transportation**: - From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅}} (North Exit), take a bus bound for {{ill|Bunkyo University|ja|文教大学}} and get off at Takata. Walk for 3 minutes. == Festivals == * **New Year's Festival**: {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}} * **Hamaori Festival**: {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日}} * **festival**: Second Sunday of {{ill|September|ja|9月}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{cite book|ja|title=Great Japan Geographical Chronicles|volume=Volume 38: New Edition of Sagami Province Topographical Records, Scroll 3|chapter=Ōba Manor, Takata Village, Kumano Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1179219/150}}|publisher=Yuzankaku|date=August 1932}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.com/mem/main/196.html Kanagawa Shrine Map - Kumano Shrine] * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0&PHPSESSID=ff8abecd9e6b1bb6e0394ca1c38c0d2f Kumano Shrine] — [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/ Kanagawa Shrine Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto template}} [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[Category:History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Buildings in Chigasaki]] [[ja:%E9%AB%98%E7%94%B0%E7%86%8A%E9%87%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E8%8C%85%E3%83%B6%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:bump 1-25]]

Walk for 3 minutes. == Festivals == * **New Year's Festival**: {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}} * **Hamaori Festival**: {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日}} * **festival**: Second Sunday of {{ill|September|ja|9月}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{cite book|ja|title=Great Japan Geographical Chronicles|volume=Volume 38: New Edition of Sagami Province Topographical Records, Scroll 3|chapter=Ōba Manor, Takata Village, Kumano Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1179219/150}}|publisher=Yuzankaku|date=August 1932}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.com/mem/main/196.html Kanagawa Shrine Map - Kumano Shrine] * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0&PHPSESSID=ff8abecd9e6b1bb6e0394ca1c38c0d2f Kumano Shrine] — [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/ Kanagawa Shrine Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto template}} [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[Category:History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Buildings in Chigasaki]] [[ja:%E9%AB%98%E7%94%B0%E7%86%8A%E9%87%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E8%8C%85%E3%83%B6%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:bump 1-25]]

It is historically linked to the family of {{ill|Ōoka Tadasuke|ja|大岡忠相}}, the famous magistrate of the Edo period. == History == (This section primarily references the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association website{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0 |title=Kumano Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2018-12-27 }}. Additional sources are noted where applicable.) Kumano Shrine was established in {{ill|Manji|ja|万治}} First Year (1658) by {{ill|Ōoka Tadataka|ja|大岡忠高}}, the headman of Takata Village in the {{ill|Kōza District|ja|高座郡}}, {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}} (father of Ōoka Tadasuke). The shrine enshrines a divided deity from the {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Muro District, Wakayama|ja|牟婁郡}}. The current shrine building was reconstructed in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 2 (1927). The subordinate shrine, {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)}}, was brought from {{ill|Hie Taisha|ja|日吉大社}} in {{ill|Sakamoto, Shiga|ja|坂本 (滋賀郡)}} in {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 5 (1720) by the Ōoka family. Originally located on a hill known as Mount Sannō, it was later moved to the grounds of Kumano Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Kumano Ōkami * Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto * Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities * Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto (Hie Shrine deity) == Subordinate Shrines == * {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)}} == Access == * **By car**: Located along Kumano Shrine Road. Parking available nearby. - Approximately 12 km south from {{ill|National Route 1 (Japan)|ja|国道1号}} Amagawa intersection. - Approximately 2 km north from {{ill|National Route 163 (Japan)|ja|国道163号}} Kitatawara Bridge intersection. * **By public transportation**: - From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅}} (North Exit), take a bus bound for {{ill|Bunkyo University|ja|文教大学}} and get off at Takata. Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto
Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities | SacredObject = | Rank = | consecration_year = {{ill|Manji|ja|万治}} First Year (1658) | MainBuilding = | Alternatenames = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = {{ill|Second Sunday of September|ja|9月第2日曜日}} | Rituals = }} '''Kumano Shrine''' (熊野神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Takata, {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is historically linked to the family of {{ill|Ōoka Tadasuke|ja|大岡忠相}}, the famous magistrate of the Edo period. == History == (This section primarily references the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association website{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0 |title=Kumano Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2018-12-27 }}. Additional sources are noted where applicable.) Kumano Shrine was established in {{ill|Manji|ja|万治}} First Year (1658) by {{ill|Ōoka Tadataka|ja|大岡忠高}}, the headman of Takata Village in the {{ill|Kōza District|ja|高座郡}}, {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}} (father of Ōoka Tadasuke). The shrine enshrines a divided deity from the {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Muro District, Wakayama|ja|牟婁郡}}. The current shrine building was reconstructed in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 2 (1927). The subordinate shrine, {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)}}, was brought from {{ill|Hie Taisha|ja|日吉大社}} in {{ill|Sakamoto, Shiga|ja|坂本 (滋賀郡)}} in {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 5 (1720) by the Ōoka family. Originally located on a hill known as Mount Sannō, it was later moved to the grounds of Kumano Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Kumano Ōkami * Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto * Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities * Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto (Hie Shrine deity) == Subordinate Shrines == * {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)}} == Access == * **By car**: Located along Kumano Shrine Road. Parking available nearby. - Approximately 12 km south from {{ill|National Route 1 (Japan)|ja|国道1号}} Amagawa intersection. - Approximately 2 km north from {{ill|National Route 163 (Japan)|ja|国道163号}} Kitatawara Bridge intersection. * **By public transportation**: - From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅}} (North Exit), take a bus bound for {{ill|Bunkyo University|ja|文教大学}} and get off at Takata. Walk for 3 minutes. == Festivals == * **New Year's Festival**: {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}} * **Hamaori Festival**: {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日}} * **festival**: Second Sunday of {{ill|September|ja|9月}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{cite book|ja|title=Great Japan Geographical Chronicles|volume=Volume 38: New Edition of Sagami Province Topographical Records, Scroll 3|chapter=Ōba Manor, Takata Village, Kumano Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1179219/150}}|publisher=Yuzankaku|date=August 1932}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.com/mem/main/196.html Kanagawa Shrine Map - Kumano Shrine] * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0&PHPSESSID=ff8abecd9e6b1bb6e0394ca1c38c0d2f Kumano Shrine] — [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/ Kanagawa Shrine Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto template}} [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[Category:History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Buildings in Chigasaki]] [[ja:%E9%AB%98%E7%94%B0%E7%86%8A%E9%87%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E8%8C%85%E3%83%B6%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)-qq]] [[Category:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)-qq]] [[Category:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqqqq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:11]]

{{Shrine | name = Kumano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kumono shrine chigasaki Kanagawa 2023 Jan 20 various 17 42 44 119000.jpeg|270px]]

Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto
Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities | SacredObject = | Rank = | consecration_year = {{ill|Manji|ja|万治}} First Year (1658) | MainBuilding = | Alternatenames = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = {{ill|Second Sunday of September|ja|9月第2日曜日}} | Rituals = }} '''Kumano Shrine''' (熊野神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Takata, {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is historically linked to the family of {{ill|Ōoka Tadasuke|ja|大岡忠相}}, the famous magistrate of the Edo period. == History == (This section primarily references the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association website{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0 |title=Kumano Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2018-12-27 }}. Additional sources are noted where applicable.) Kumano Shrine was established in {{ill|Manji|ja|万治}} First Year (1658) by {{ill|Ōoka Tadataka|ja|大岡忠高}}, the headman of Takata Village in the {{ill|Kōza District|ja|高座郡}}, {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}} (father of Ōoka Tadasuke). The shrine enshrines a divided deity from the {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Muro District, Wakayama|ja|牟婁郡}}. The current shrine building was reconstructed in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 2 (1927). The subordinate shrine, {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)}}, was brought from {{ill|Hie Taisha|ja|日吉大社}} in {{ill|Sakamoto, Shiga|ja|坂本 (滋賀郡)}} in {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 5 (1720) by the Ōoka family. Originally located on a hill known as Mount Sannō, it was later moved to the grounds of Kumano Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Kumano Ōkami * Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto * Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities * Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto (Hie Shrine deity) == Subordinate Shrines == * {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)}} == Access == * **By car**: Located along Kumano Shrine Road. Walk for 3 minutes. == Festivals == * **New Year's Festival**: {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}} * **Hamaori Festival**: {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日}} * **festival**: Second Sunday of {{ill|September|ja|9月}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{cite book|ja|title=Great Japan Geographical Chronicles|volume=Volume 38: New Edition of Sagami Province Topographical Records, Scroll 3|chapter=Ōba Manor, Takata Village, Kumano Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1179219/150}}|publisher=Yuzankaku|date=August 1932}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.com/mem/main/196.html Kanagawa Shrine Map - Kumano Shrine] * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0&PHPSESSID=ff8abecd9e6b1bb6e0394ca1c38c0d2f Kumano Shrine] — [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/ Kanagawa Shrine Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto template}} [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[Category:History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Buildings in Chigasaki]] [[ja:%E9%AB%98%E7%94%B0%E7%86%8A%E9%87%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E8%8C%85%E3%83%B6%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)-qq]] [[Category:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)-qq]] [[Category:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqqqq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:11]]

Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto
Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities | SacredObject = | Rank = | consecration_year = {{ill|Manji|ja|万治}} First Year (1658) | MainBuilding = | Alternatenames = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = {{ill|Second Sunday of September|ja|9月第2日曜日}} | Rituals = }} '''Kumano Shrine''' (熊野神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Takata, {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is historically linked to the family of {{ill|Ōoka Tadasuke|ja|大岡忠相}}, the famous magistrate of the Edo period. == History == (This section primarily references the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association website{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0 |title=Kumano Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2018-12-27 }}. Additional sources are noted where applicable.) Kumano Shrine was established in {{ill|Manji|ja|万治}} First Year (1658) by {{ill|Ōoka Tadataka|ja|大岡忠高}}, the headman of Takata Village in the {{ill|Kōza District|ja|高座郡}}, {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}} (father of Ōoka Tadasuke). The shrine enshrines a divided deity from the {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Muro District, Wakayama|ja|牟婁郡}}. The current shrine building was reconstructed in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 2 (1927). The subordinate shrine, {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)}}, was brought from {{ill|Hie Taisha|ja|日吉大社}} in {{ill|Sakamoto, Shiga|ja|坂本 (滋賀郡)}} in {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 5 (1720) by the Ōoka family. Originally located on a hill known as Mount Sannō, it was later moved to the grounds of Kumano Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Kumano Ōkami * Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto * Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities * Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto (Hie Shrine deity) == Subordinate Shrines == * {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)}} == Access == * '''By car''': Located along Kumano Shrine Road. Parking available nearby. - Approximately 12 km south from {{ill|National Route 1 (Japan)|ja|国道1号}} Amagawa intersection. - Approximately 2 km north from {{ill|National Route 163 (Japan)|ja|国道163号}} Kitatawara Bridge intersection. * '''By public transportation''': - From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅}} (North Exit), take a bus bound for {{ill|Bunkyo University|ja|文教大学}} and get off at Takata. {{Shrine | name = Kumano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kumono shrine chigasaki Kanagawa 2023 Jan 20 various 17 42 44 119000.jpeg|270px]]
Torii and Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|55.6|N|139|24|54.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}} Takata 1-10-44 | LatitudeDeg = 35 | LatitudeMin = 20 | LatitudeSec = 55.6 | NS = N | LongitudeDeg = 139 | LongitudeMin = 24 | LongitudeSec = 54.6 | EW = E | CountryCode = JP | Map = Japan Kanagawa#Japan | Deities = Kumano Ōkami
Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto
Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities | SacredObject = | Rank = | consecration_year = {{ill|Manji|ja|万治}} First Year (1658) | MainBuilding = | Alternatenames = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = {{ill|Second Sunday of September|ja|9月第2日曜日}} | Rituals = }} '''Kumano Shrine''' (熊野神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Takata, {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is historically linked to the family of {{ill|Ōoka Tadasuke|ja|大岡忠相}}, the famous magistrate of the Edo period. == History == (This section primarily references the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association website{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0 |title=Kumano Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2018-12-27 }}. Additional sources are noted where applicable.) Kumano Shrine was established in {{ill|Manji|ja|万治}} First Year (1658) by {{ill|Ōoka Tadataka|ja|大岡忠高}}, the headman of Takata Village in the {{ill|Kōza District|ja|高座郡}}, {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}} (father of Ōoka Tadasuke). The shrine enshrines a divided deity from the {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Muro District, Wakayama|ja|牟婁郡}}. The current shrine building was reconstructed in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 2 (1927). The subordinate shrine, {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)}}, was brought from {{ill|Hie Taisha|ja|日吉大社}} in {{ill|Sakamoto, Shiga|ja|坂本 (滋賀郡)}} in {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 5 (1720) by the Ōoka family. Originally located on a hill known as Mount Sannō, it was later moved to the grounds of Kumano Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Kumano Ōkami * Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto * Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities * Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto (Hie Shrine deity) == Subordinate Shrines == * {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)}} == Access == * '''By car''': Located along Kumano Shrine Road. Parking available nearby. - Approximately 12 km south from {{ill|National Route 1 (Japan)|ja|国道1号}} Amagawa intersection. - Approximately 2 km north from {{ill|National Route 163 (Japan)|ja|国道163号}} Kitatawara Bridge intersection. * '''By public transportation''': - From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅}} (North Exit), take a bus bound for {{ill|Bunkyo University|ja|文教大学}} and get off at Takata. Walk for 3 minutes. == Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''': {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}} * '''Hamaori Festival''': {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日}} * '''festival''': Second Sunday of {{ill|September|ja|9月}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{cite book|ja|title=Great Japan Geographical Chronicles|volume=Volume 38: New Edition of Sagami Province Topographical Records, Scroll 3|chapter=Ōba Manor, Takata Village, Kumano Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1179219/150}}|publisher=Yuzankaku|date=August 1932}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.com/mem/main/196.html Kanagawa Shrine Map - Kumano Shrine] * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0&PHPSESSID=ff8abecd9e6b1bb6e0394ca1c38c0d2f Kumano Shrine] — [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/ Kanagawa Shrine Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto template}} [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[Category:History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Buildings in Chigasaki]] [[ja:%E9%AB%98%E7%94%B0%E7%86%8A%E9%87%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E8%8C%85%E3%83%B6%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Ukkkk Completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:Ukkkk Completion categories]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqqqq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:akada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:New]]

{{Shrine | name = Kumano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kumono shrine chigasaki Kanagawa 2023 Jan 20 various 17 42 44 119000.jpeg|270px]]
Torii and Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|55.6|N|139|24|54.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}} Takata 1-10-44 | LatitudeDeg = 35 | LatitudeMin = 20 | LatitudeSec = 55.6 | NS = N | LongitudeDeg = 139 | LongitudeMin = 24 | LongitudeSec = 54.6 | EW = E | CountryCode = JP | Map = Japan Kanagawa#Japan | Deities = Kumano Ōkami
Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto
Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities | SacredObject = | Rank = | consecration_year = {{ill|Manji|ja|万治}} First Year (1658) | MainBuilding = | Alternatenames = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = {{ill|Second Sunday of September|ja|9月第2日曜日}} | Rituals = }} '''Kumano Shrine''' (熊野神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Takata, {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is historically linked to the family of {{ill|Ōoka Tadasuke|ja|大岡忠相}}, the famous magistrate of the Edo period. == History == (This section primarily references the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association website{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0 |title=Kumano Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2018-12-27 }}. Additional sources are noted where applicable.) Kumano Shrine was established in {{ill|Manji|ja|万治}} First Year (1658) by {{ill|Ōoka Tadataka|ja|大岡忠高}}, the headman of Takata Village in the {{ill|Kōza District|ja|高座郡}}, {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}} (father of Ōoka Tadasuke). The shrine enshrines a divided deity from the {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Muro District, Wakayama|ja|牟婁郡}}. The current shrine building was reconstructed in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 2 (1927). The subordinate shrine, {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)}}, was brought from {{ill|Hie Taisha|ja|日吉大社}} in {{ill|Sakamoto, Shiga|ja|坂本 (滋賀郡)}} in {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 5 (1720) by the Ōoka family. Originally located on a hill known as Mount Sannō, it was later moved to the grounds of Kumano Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Kumano Ōkami * Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto * Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities * Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto (Hie Shrine deity) == Subordinate Shrines == * {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)}} == Access == * '''By car''': Located along Kumano Shrine Road. Parking available nearby. - Approximately 12 km south from {{ill|National Route 1 (Japan)|ja|国道1号}} Amagawa intersection. - Approximately 2 km north from {{ill|National Route 163 (Japan)|ja|国道163号}} Kitatawara Bridge intersection. * '''By public transportation''': - From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅}} (North Exit), take a bus bound for {{ill|Bunkyo University|ja|文教大学}} and get off at Takata. Walk for 3 minutes. == Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''': {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}} * '''Hamaori Festival''': {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日}} * '''festival''': Second Sunday of {{ill|September|ja|9月}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{cite book|ja|title=Great Japan Geographical Chronicles|volume=Volume 38: New Edition of Sagami Province Topographical Records, Scroll 3|chapter=Ōba Manor, Takata Village, Kumano Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1179219/150}}|publisher=Yuzankaku|date=August 1932}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.com/mem/main/196.html Kanagawa Shrine Map - Kumano Shrine] * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0&PHPSESSID=ff8abecd9e6b1bb6e0394ca1c38c0d2f Kumano Shrine] — [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/ Kanagawa Shrine Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto template}} [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[Category:History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Buildings in Chigasaki]] [[ja:%E9%AB%98%E7%94%B0%E7%86%8A%E9%87%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E8%8C%85%E3%83%B6%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Ukkkk Completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:Ukkkk Completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqqqq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:akada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 3]] [[Category:New]]

It is historically linked to the family of {{ill|Ōoka Tadasuke|ja|大岡忠相}}, the famous magistrate of the Edo period. == History == (This section primarily references the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association website{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0 |title=Kumano Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2018-12-27 }}. Additional sources are noted where applicable.) Kumano Shrine was established in {{ill|Manji|ja|万治}} First Year (1658) by {{ill|Ōoka Tadataka|ja|大岡忠高}}, the headman of Takata Village in the {{ill|Kōza District|ja|高座郡}}, {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}} (father of Ōoka Tadasuke). The shrine enshrines a divided deity from the {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Muro District, Wakayama|ja|牟婁郡}}. The current shrine building was reconstructed in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 2 (1927). The subordinate shrine, {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)}}, was brought from {{ill|Hie Taisha|ja|日吉大社}} in {{ill|Sakamoto, Shiga|ja|坂本 (滋賀郡)}} in {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 5 (1720) by the Ōoka family. Originally located on a hill known as Mount Sannō, it was later moved to the grounds of Kumano Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Kumano Ōkami * Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto * Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities * Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto (Hie Shrine deity) == Subordinate Shrines == * {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)}} == Access == * '''By car''': Located along Kumano Shrine Road. It is historically linked to the family of {{ill|Ōoka Tadasuke|ja|大岡忠相}}, the famous magistrate of the Edo period. == History == (This section primarily references the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association website{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0 |title=Kumano Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2018-12-27 }}. Additional sources are noted where applicable.) Kumano Shrine was established in {{ill|Manji|ja|万治}} First Year (1658) by {{ill|Ōoka Tadataka|ja|大岡忠高}}, the headman of Takata Village in the {{ill|Kōza District|ja|高座郡}}, {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}} (father of Ōoka Tadasuke). The shrine enshrines a divided deity from the {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Muro District, Wakayama|ja|牟婁郡}}. The current shrine building was reconstructed in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 2 (1927). The subordinate shrine, {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)}}, was brought from {{ill|Hie Taisha|ja|日吉大社}} in {{ill|Sakamoto, Shiga|ja|坂本 (滋賀郡)}} in {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 5 (1720) by the Ōoka family. Originally located on a hill known as Mount Sannō, it was later moved to the grounds of Kumano Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Kumano Ōkami * Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto * Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities * Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto (Hie Shrine deity) == Subordinate Shrines == * {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)}} == Access == * '''By car''': Located along Kumano Shrine Road. It is historically linked to the family of {{ill|Ōoka Tadasuke|ja|大岡忠相}}, the famous magistrate of the Edo period. == History == (This section primarily references the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association website{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0 |title=Kumano Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2018-12-27 }}. Additional sources are noted where applicable.) Kumano Shrine was established in {{ill|Manji|ja|万治}} First Year (1658) by {{ill|Ōoka Tadataka|ja|大岡忠高}}, the headman of Takata Village in the {{ill|Kōza District|ja|高座郡}}, {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}} (father of Ōoka Tadasuke). The shrine enshrines a divided deity from the {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Muro District, Wakayama|ja|牟婁郡}}. The current shrine building was reconstructed in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 2 (1927). The subordinate shrine, {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)}}, was brought from {{ill|Hie Taisha|ja|日吉大社}} in {{ill|Sakamoto, Shiga|ja|坂本 (滋賀郡)}} in {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 5 (1720) by the Ōoka family. Originally located on a hill known as Mount Sannō, it was later moved to the grounds of Kumano Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Kumano Ōkami * Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto * Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities * Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto (Hie Shrine deity) == Subordinate Shrines == * {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)}} == Access == * '''By car''': Located along Kumano Shrine Road. Kumano Shrine] * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0&PHPSESSID=ff8abecd9e6b1bb6e0394ca1c38c0d2f Kumano Shrine] — [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/ Kanagawa Shrine Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto template}} [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[Category:History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Buildings in Chigasaki]] [[ja:%E9%AB%98%E7%94%B0%E7%86%8A%E9%87%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E8%8C%85%E3%83%B6%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]] [[Category:Ukkkk Completion tier 4]][[Category: categories]] [[Category:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:Ukkkk Completion tier 4]][[Category: categories]] [[Category:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:akada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]][[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

{{Shrine | name = Kumano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kumono shrine chigasaki Kanagawa 2023 Jan 20 various 17 42 44 119000.jpeg|270px]]
Torii and Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|55.6|N|139|24|54.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}} {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}} Takata 1-10-44 | LatitudeDeg = 35 | LatitudeMin = 20 | LatitudeSec = 55.6 | NS = N | LongitudeDeg = 139 | LongitudeMin = 24 | LongitudeSec = 54.6 | EW = E | CountryCode = JP | Map = Japan Kanagawa#Japan | Deities = Kumano Ōkami
Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto
Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities | SacredObject = | Rank = | consecration_year = {{ill|Manji|ja|万治}} First Year (1658) | MainBuilding = | Alternatenames = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = {{ill|Second Sunday of September|ja|9月第2日曜日}} | Rituals = }} '''Kumano Shrine''' (熊野神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Takata, {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}. It is historically linked to the family of {{ill|Ōoka Tadasuke|ja|大岡忠相}}, the famous magistrate of the Edo period. == History == (This section primarily references the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association website{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0 |title=Kumano Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2018-12-27 }}. Additional sources are noted where applicable.) Kumano Shrine was established in {{ill|Manji|ja|万治}} First Year (1658) by {{ill|Ōoka Tadataka|ja|大岡忠高}}, the headman of Takata Village in the {{ill|Kōza District|ja|高座郡}}, {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国}} (father of Ōoka Tadasuke). The shrine enshrines a divided deity from the {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社}} in {{ill|Muro District, Wakayama|ja|牟婁郡}}. The current shrine building was reconstructed in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 2 (1927). The subordinate shrine, {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)}}, was brought from {{ill|Hie Taisha|ja|日吉大社}} in {{ill|Sakamoto, Shiga|ja|坂本 (滋賀郡)}} in {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保}} 5 (1720) by the Ōoka family. Originally located on a hill known as Mount Sannō, it was later moved to the grounds of Kumano Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Kumano Ōkami * Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto * Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities * Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto (Hie Shrine deity) == Subordinate Shrines == * {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)}} == Access == * '''By car''': Located along Kumano Shrine Road. Parking available nearby. ** Approximately 12 km south from {{ill|National Route 1 (Japan)|ja|国道1号}} Amagawa intersection. ** Approximately 2 km north from {{ill|National Route 163 (Japan)|ja|国道163号}} Kitatawara Bridge intersection. * '''By public transportation''': ** From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅}} (North Exit), take a bus bound for {{ill|Bunkyo University|ja|文教大学}} and get off at Takata. Walk for 3 minutes. == Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''': {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}} * '''Hamaori Festival''': {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日}} * '''festival''': Second Sunday of {{ill|September|ja|9月}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{cite book|ja|title=Great Japan Geographical Chronicles|volume=Volume 38: New Edition of Sagami Province Topographical Records, Scroll 3|chapter=Ōba Manor, Takata Village, Kumano Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1179219/150}}|publisher=Yuzankaku|date=August 1932}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.com/mem/main/196.html Kanagawa Shrine Map - Walk for 3 minutes. == Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''': {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日}} * '''Hamaori Festival''': {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日}} * '''festival''': Second Sunday of {{ill|September|ja|9月}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{cite book|ja|title=Great Japan Geographical Chronicles|volume=Volume 38: New Edition of Sagami Province Topographical Records, Scroll 3|chapter=Ōba Manor, Takata Village, Kumano Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1179219/150}}|publisher=Yuzankaku|date=August 1932}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.com/mem/main/196.html Kanagawa Shrine Map - Kumano Shrine] * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0&PHPSESSID=ff8abecd9e6b1bb6e0394ca1c38c0d2f Kumano Shrine] — [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/ Kanagawa Shrine Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto template}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[Category:History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Buildings in Chigasaki]] [[Category:Ukkkk Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:%E9%AB%98%E7%94%B0%E7%86%8A%E9%87%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E8%8C%85%E3%83%B6%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]] [[sn:akada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]]

{{Shrine | name = Kumano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kumono shrine chigasaki Kanagawa 2023 Jan 20 various 17 42 44 119000.jpeg|270px]]
Torii and Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|55.6|N|139|24|54.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft}} {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市|12=draft}} Takata 1-10-44 | LatitudeDeg = 35 | LatitudeMin = 20 | LatitudeSec = 55.6 | NS = N | LongitudeDeg = 139 | LongitudeMin = 24 | LongitudeSec = 54.6 | EW = E | CountryCode = JP | Map = Japan Kanagawa#Japan | Deities = Kumano Ōkami

Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities | SacredObject = | Rank = | consecration_year = {{ill|Manji|ja|万治|12=draft}} First Year (1658) | MainBuilding = | Alternatenames = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = {{ill|Second Sunday of September|ja|9月第2日曜日|12=draft}} | Rituals = }} '''Kumano Shrine''' (熊野神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Takata, {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|12=draft}}. It is historically linked to the family of {{ill|Ōoka Tadasuke|ja|大岡忠相|12=draft}}, the famous magistrate of the Edo period. == History == (This section primarily references the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association website{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0 |title=Kumano Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2018-12-27 }}. Additional sources are noted where applicable.) Kumano Shrine was established in {{ill|Manji|ja|万治|12=draft}} First Year (1658) by {{ill|Ōoka Tadataka|ja|大岡忠高|12=draft}}, the headman of Takata Village in the {{ill|Kōza District|ja|高座郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国|12=draft}} (father of Ōoka Tadasuke). The shrine enshrines a divided deity from the {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Muro District, Wakayama|ja|牟婁郡|12=draft}}. The current shrine building was reconstructed in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|12=draft}} 2 (1927). The subordinate shrine, {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|12=draft}}, was brought from {{ill|Hie Taisha|ja|日吉大社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Sakamoto, Shiga|ja|坂本 (滋賀郡)|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保|12=draft}} 5 (1720) by the Ōoka family. Originally located on a hill known as Mount Sannō, it was later moved to the grounds of Kumano Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Kumano Ōkami * Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto * Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities * Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto (Hie Shrine deity) == Subordinate Shrines == * {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|12=draft}} == Access == * '''By car''': Located along Kumano Shrine Road. Parking available nearby. ** Approximately 12 km south from {{ill|National Route 1 (Japan)|ja|国道1号|12=draft}} Amagawa intersection. ** Approximately 2 km north from {{ill|National Route 163 (Japan)|ja|国道163号|12=draft}} Kitatawara Bridge intersection. * '''By public transportation''': ** From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅|12=draft}} (North Exit), take a bus bound for {{ill|Bunkyo University|ja|文教大学|12=draft}} and get off at Takata. Walk for 3 minutes. == Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''': {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日|12=draft}} * '''Hamaori Festival''': {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日|12=draft}} * '''festival''': Second Sunday of {{ill|September|ja|9月|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{cite book|ja|title=Great Japan Geographical Chronicles|volume=Volume 38: New Edition of Sagami Province Topographical Records, Scroll 3|chapter=Ōba Manor, Takata Village, Kumano Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1179219/150}}|publisher=Yuzankaku|date=August 1932}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.com/mem/main/196.html Kanagawa Shrine Map - Kumano Shrine] * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0&PHPSESSID=ff8abecd9e6b1bb6e0394ca1c38c0d2f Kumano Shrine] — [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/ Kanagawa Shrine Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto template}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[Category:History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Buildings in Chigasaki]] [[Category:Ukkkk Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:%E9%AB%98%E7%94%B0%E7%86%8A%E9%87%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E8%8C%85%E3%83%B6%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]] [[sn:akada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]]

It is historically linked to the family of {{ill|Ōoka Tadasuke|ja|大岡忠相|12=draft|12=draft}}, the famous magistrate of the Edo period. == History == (This section primarily references the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association website{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0 |title=Kumano Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2018-12-27 }}. Additional sources are noted where applicable.) Kumano Shrine was established in {{ill|Manji|ja|万治|12=draft|12=draft}} First Year (1658) by {{ill|Ōoka Tadataka|ja|大岡忠高|12=draft|12=draft}}, the headman of Takata Village in the {{ill|Kōza District|ja|高座郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国|12=draft|12=draft}} (father of Ōoka Tadasuke). The shrine enshrines a divided deity from the {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Muro District, Wakayama|ja|牟婁郡|12=draft|12=draft}}. The current shrine building was reconstructed in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|12=draft|12=draft}} 2 (1927). The subordinate shrine, {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|12=draft|12=draft}}, was brought from {{ill|Hie Taisha|ja|日吉大社|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Sakamoto, Shiga|ja|坂本 (滋賀郡)|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5 (1720) by the Ōoka family. Originally located on a hill known as Mount Sannō, it was later moved to the grounds of Kumano Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Kumano Ōkami * Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto * Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities * Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto (Hie Shrine deity) == Subordinate Shrines == * {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * '''By car''': Located along Kumano Shrine Road. Walk for 3 minutes. == Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''': {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Hamaori Festival''': {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''festival''': Second Sunday of {{ill|September|ja|9月|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{cite book|ja|title=Great Japan Geographical Chronicles|volume=Volume 38: New Edition of Sagami Province Topographical Records, Scroll 3|chapter=Ōba Manor, Takata Village, Kumano Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1179219/150}}|publisher=Yuzankaku|date=August 1932}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.com/mem/main/196.html Kanagawa Shrine Map - Kumano Shrine] * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0&PHPSESSID=ff8abecd9e6b1bb6e0394ca1c38c0d2f Kumano Shrine] — [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/ Kanagawa Shrine Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto template}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[Category:History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Buildings in Chigasaki]] [[Category:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:%E9%AB%98%E7%94%B0%E7%86%8A%E9%87%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E8%8C%85%E3%83%B6%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]] [[sn:akada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]]

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 熊野神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Kumono shrine chigasaki Kanagawa 2023 Jan 20 various 17 42 44 119000.jpeg|270px]]
鳥居と拝殿 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|55.6|N|139|24|54.6|E}}}} |所在地 = [[神奈川県]][[茅ヶ崎市]]高田1-10-44 | 緯度度 = 35 | 緯度分 = 20 | 緯度秒 = 55.6 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 139 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 54.6 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Kanagawa#Japan |祭神 = 熊野大神
櫛御気野命
天神七座地神五座 |神体 = |社格 = |創建 = [[万治]]元年（[[1658年]]） |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[9月]]第2[[日曜日]] |神事 = }} '''熊野神社'''（くまのじんじゃ）は[[神奈川県]][[茅ヶ崎市]]高田にある[[神社]]。[[大岡忠相|大岡越前守忠相]]の一族ゆかりの[[神社]]。 == 由緒 == （由緒節の主要な出典は神奈川県神社庁ウェブサイト{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0 |title=熊野神社 |publisher=神奈川県神社庁 |accessdate=2018-12-27 }}） 熊野神社は当時の[[相模国]][[高座郡]]高田村の[[地頭]]であった[[大岡忠高|大岡美濃守忠高]]（大岡忠相の実父）が[[万治]]元年（[[1658年]]）に[[紀伊国]][[牟婁郡]]の[[熊野本宮大社]]から高田村に分祀し、'''熊野神社'''を創建したといわれる。現在の社殿は[[昭和]]2年（[[1927年]]）に再建されたもの。 摂末社の日枝神社は[[享保]]5年（[[1720年]]）、[[大岡氏]]が[[近江国]][[滋賀郡]]坂本の[[日吉大社]]から[[勧請]]されたもので、古くは山王山と呼ばれる丘隆地に建てられていたが、のちに熊野神社境内に移された。 == 祭神 == * 熊野大神 * 櫛御気野命 * 天神七座地神五座 * 大山咋命（日枝神社祭神） == 境内社 == * [[日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|日枝神社]] == 交通 == * [[東日本旅客鉄道|JR東日本]]：[[茅ケ崎駅]]北口より、[[文教大学]]行きバスに乗り、高田で下車。徒歩3分。 == 祭事 == * 歳旦祭（年賀祭）：[[1月1日]] * [[浜降祭]]：[[7月14日]] * [[例祭]]：[[9月]]第2[[日曜日]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連文献 == * {{cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第38巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]3巻之61村里部高座郡巻之3|chapter=大庭庄 高田村 熊野社|id={{NDLJP|1179219/150}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.com/mem/main/196.html 神奈川県神社マップ - 熊野神社] * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0&PHPSESSID=ff8abecd9e6b1bb6e0394ca1c38c0d2f 熊野神社] — [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/ 神奈川県神社庁] {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たかたくまのしんしや}} [[Category:神奈川県の神社]] [[Category:熊野神社|たかたくまのしんしや ちかさきし]] [[Category:茅ヶ崎市の歴史]] [[Category:茅ヶ崎市の建築物]]

{{Shrine | name = Kumano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kumono shrine chigasaki Kanagawa 2023 Jan 20 various 17 42 44 119000.jpeg|270px]]
Torii and Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|55.6|N|139|24|54.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Takata 1-10-44 | LatitudeDeg = 35 | LatitudeMin = 20 | LatitudeSec = 55.6 | NS = N | LongitudeDeg = 139 | LongitudeMin = 24 | LongitudeSec = 54.6 | EW = E | CountryCode = JP | Map = Japan Kanagawa#Japan | Deities = Kumano Ōkami
Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto
Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities | SacredObject = | Rank = | consecration_year = {{ill|Manji|ja|万治|qq=draft|qq=draft}} First Year (1658) | MainBuilding = | Alternatenames = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = {{ill|Second Sunday of September|ja|9月第2日曜日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Rituals = }} '''Kumano Shrine''' (熊野神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Takata, {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is historically linked to the family of {{ill|Ōoka Tadasuke|ja|大岡忠相|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the famous magistrate of the Edo period. == History == (This section primarily references the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association website{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0 |title=Kumano Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2018-12-27 }}. Additional sources are noted where applicable.) Kumano Shrine was established in {{ill|Manji|ja|万治|qq=draft|qq=draft}} First Year (1658) by {{ill|Ōoka Tadataka|ja|大岡忠高|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the headman of Takata Village in the {{ill|Kōza District|ja|高座郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (father of Ōoka Tadasuke). The shrine enshrines a divided deity from the {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Muro District, Wakayama|ja|牟婁郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The current shrine building was reconstructed in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (1927). The subordinate shrine, {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was brought from {{ill|Hie Taisha|ja|日吉大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Sakamoto, Shiga|ja|坂本 (滋賀郡)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (1720) by the Ōoka family. Originally located on a hill known as Mount Sannō, it was later moved to the grounds of Kumano Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Kumano Ōkami * Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto * Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities * Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto (Hie Shrine deity) == Subordinate Shrines == * {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Access == * '''By car''': Located along Kumano Shrine Road. Parking available nearby. ** Approximately 12 km south from {{ill|National Route 1 (Japan)|ja|国道1号|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Amagawa intersection. ** Approximately 2 km north from {{ill|National Route 163 (Japan)|ja|国道163号|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kitatawara Bridge intersection. * '''By public transportation''': ** From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (North Exit), take a bus bound for {{ill|Bunkyo University|ja|文教大学|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and get off at Takata. Walk for 3 minutes. == Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''': {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Hamaori Festival''': {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''festival''': Second Sunday of {{ill|September|ja|9月|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{cite book|ja|title=Great Japan Geographical Chronicles|volume=Volume 38: New Edition of Sagami Province Topographical Records, Scroll 3|chapter=Ōba Manor, Takata Village, Kumano Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1179219/150}}|publisher=Yuzankaku|date=August 1932}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.com/mem/main/196.html Kanagawa Shrine Map - Kumano Shrine] * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0&PHPSESSID=ff8abecd9e6b1bb6e0394ca1c38c0d2f Kumano Shrine] — [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/ Kanagawa Shrine Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto template}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[Category:History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Buildings in Chigasaki]] [[Category:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:%E9%AB%98%E7%94%B0%E7%86%8A%E9%87%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E8%8C%85%E3%83%B6%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]] [[sn:akada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]] {{translated page|ja|高田熊野神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|version=98347473|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities | SacredObject = | Rank = | consecration_year = {{ill|Manji|ja|万治|qq=draft|qq=draft}} First Year (1658) | MainBuilding = | Alternatenames = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = {{ill|Second Sunday of September|ja|9月第2日曜日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Rituals = }} '''Kumano Shrine''' (熊野神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Takata, {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is historically linked to the family of {{ill|Ōoka Tadasuke|ja|大岡忠相|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the famous magistrate of the Edo period. == History == (This section primarily references the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association website{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0 |title=Kumano Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2018-12-27 }}. Additional sources are noted where applicable.) Kumano Shrine was established in {{ill|Manji|ja|万治|qq=draft|qq=draft}} First Year (1658) by {{ill|Ōoka Tadataka|ja|大岡忠高|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the headman of Takata Village in the {{ill|Kōza District|ja|高座郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (father of Ōoka Tadasuke). The shrine enshrines a divided deity from the {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Muro District, Wakayama|ja|牟婁郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The current shrine building was reconstructed in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (1927). The subordinate shrine, {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was brought from {{ill|Hie Taisha|ja|日吉大社|1=Hiyoshi Taisha|12=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Sakamoto, Shiga|ja|坂本 (滋賀郡)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (1720) by the Ōoka family. Originally located on a hill known as Mount Sannō, it was later moved to the grounds of Kumano Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Kumano Ōkami * Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto * Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities * Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto (Hie Shrine deity) == Subordinate Shrines == * {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Access == * '''By car''': Located along Kumano Shrine Road. Parking available nearby. ** Approximately 12 km south from {{ill|National Route 1 (Japan)|ja|国道1号|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Amagawa intersection. ** Approximately 2 km north from {{ill|National Route 163 (Japan)|ja|国道163号|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kitatawara Bridge intersection. * '''By public transportation''': ** From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (North Exit), take a bus bound for {{ill|Bunkyo University|ja|文教大学|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and get off at Takata. Walk for 3 minutes. == Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''': {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Hamaori Festival''': {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''festival''': Second Sunday of {{ill|September|ja|9月|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{cite book|ja|title=Great Japan Geographical Chronicles|volume=Volume 38: New Edition of Sagami Province Topographical Records, Scroll 3|chapter=Ōba Manor, Takata Village, Kumano Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1179219/150}}|publisher=Yuzankaku|date=August 1932}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.com/mem/main/196.html Kanagawa Shrine Map - Kumano Shrine] * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0&PHPSESSID=ff8abecd9e6b1bb6e0394ca1c38c0d2f Kumano Shrine] — [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/ Kanagawa Shrine Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto template}} {{translated page|ja|高田熊野神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|version=98347473|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[Category:History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Buildings in Chigasaki]] [[Category:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:%E9%AB%98%E7%94%B0%E7%86%8A%E9%87%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E8%8C%85%E3%83%B6%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]] [[sn:akada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]]
