Walk for 3 minutes. == Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''': {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Hamaori Festival''': {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''festival''': Second Sunday of {{ill|September|ja|9月|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{cite book|ja|title=Great Japan Geographical Chronicles|volume=Volume 38: New Edition of Sagami Province Topographical Records, Scroll 3|chapter=Ōba Manor, Takata Village, Kumano Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1179219/150}}|publisher=Yuzankaku|date=August 1932}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.com/mem/main/196.html Kanagawa Shrine Map - Kumano Shrine] * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0&PHPSESSID=ff8abecd9e6b1bb6e0394ca1c38c0d2f Kumano Shrine] — [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/ Kanagawa Shrine Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto template}} {{translated page|ja|高田熊野神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|version=98347473|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[Category:History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Buildings in Chigasaki]] [[Category:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:%E9%AB%98%E7%94%B0%E7%86%8A%E9%87%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E8%8C%85%E3%83%B6%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]] [[sn:akada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]]

{{Shrine | name = Kumano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kumono shrine chigasaki Kanagawa 2023 Jan 20 various 17 42 44 119000.jpeg|270px]]
Torii and Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|55.6|N|139|24|54.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Takata 1-10-44 | LatitudeDeg = 35 | LatitudeMin = 20 | LatitudeSec = 55.6 | NS = N | LongitudeDeg = 139 | LongitudeMin = 24 | LongitudeSec = 54.6 | EW = E | CountryCode = JP | Map = Japan Kanagawa#Japan | Deities = Kumano Ōkami
Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto
Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities | SacredObject = | Rank = | consecration_year = {{ill|Manji|ja|万治|qq=draft|qq=draft}} First Year (1658) | MainBuilding = | Alternatenames = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = {{ill|Second Sunday of September|ja|9月第2日曜日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Rituals = }} '''Kumano Shrine''' (熊野神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Takata, {{ill|Chigasaki|ja|茅ヶ崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is historically linked to the family of {{ill|Ōoka Tadasuke|ja|大岡忠相|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the famous magistrate of the Edo period. == History == (This section primarily references the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association website{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0 |title=Kumano Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2018-12-27 }}. Additional sources are noted where applicable.) Kumano Shrine was established in {{ill|Manji|ja|万治|qq=draft|qq=draft}} First Year (1658) by {{ill|Ōoka Tadataka|ja|大岡忠高|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the headman of Takata Village in the {{ill|Kōza District|ja|高座郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sagami Province|ja|相模国|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (father of Ōoka Tadasuke). The shrine enshrines a divided deity from the {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|ja|熊野本宮大社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Muro District, Wakayama|ja|牟婁郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The current shrine building was reconstructed in {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 2 (1927). The subordinate shrine, {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was brought from {{ill|Hie Taisha|ja|日吉大社|1=Hiyoshi Taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Sakamoto, Shiga|ja|坂本 (滋賀郡)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Kyōhō|ja|享保|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5 (1720) by the Ōoka family. Originally located on a hill known as Mount Sannō, it was later moved to the grounds of Kumano Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Kumano Ōkami * Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto * Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities * Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto (Hie Shrine deity) == Subordinate Shrines == * {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Access == * '''By car''': Located along Kumano Shrine Road. Parking available nearby. ** Approximately 12 km south from {{ill|National Route 1 (Japan)|ja|国道1号|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Amagawa intersection. ** Approximately 2 km north from {{ill|National Route 163 (Japan)|ja|国道163号|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kitatawara Bridge intersection. * '''By public transportation''': ** From {{ill|JR East|ja|東日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Chigasaki Station|ja|茅ケ崎駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (North Exit), take a bus bound for {{ill|Bunkyo University|ja|文教大学|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and get off at Takata. Walk for 3 minutes. == Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''': {{ill|January 1|ja|1月1日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''Hamaori Festival''': {{ill|July 14|ja|7月14日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * '''festival''': Second Sunday of {{ill|September|ja|9月|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{cite book|ja|title=Great Japan Geographical Chronicles|volume=Volume 38: New Edition of Sagami Province Topographical Records, Scroll 3|chapter=Ōba Manor, Takata Village, Kumano Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1179219/150}}|publisher=Yuzankaku|date=August 1932}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.com/mem/main/196.html Kanagawa Shrine Map - Kumano Shrine] * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0&PHPSESSID=ff8abecd9e6b1bb6e0394ca1c38c0d2f Kumano Shrine] — [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/ Kanagawa Shrine Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto template}} {{translated page|ja|高田熊野神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|version=98347473|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[Category:History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Buildings in Chigasaki]] [[Category:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:%E9%AB%98%E7%94%B0%E7%86%8A%E9%87%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E8%8C%85%E3%83%B6%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]] [[sn:akada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]]

Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities | SacredObject = | Rank = | consecration_year = [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji]] First Year (1658) | MainBuilding = | Alternatenames = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = {{ill|Second Sunday of September|ja|9月第2日曜日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Sunday of September|qq=}} | Rituals = }} '''Kumano Shrine''' (熊野神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Takata, [[:en:Chigasaki, Kanagawa|Chigasaki]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is historically linked to the family of [[:en:Ōoka Tadasuke|Ōoka Tadasuke]], the famous magistrate of the Edo period. == History == (This section primarily references the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association website{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0 |title=Kumano Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2018-12-27 }}. Additional sources are noted where applicable.) Kumano Shrine was established in [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji]] First Year (1658) by {{ill|Ōoka Tadataka|ja|大岡忠高|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōoka Tadataka|qq=}}, the headman of Takata Village in the [[:en:Kōza District, Kanagawa|Kōza District]], [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]] (father of Ōoka Tadasuke). The shrine enshrines a divided deity from the [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] in [[:en:Muro District, Kii|Muro District, Wakayama]]. The current shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 2 (1927). The subordinate shrine, {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|qq=}}, was brought from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] in {{ill|Sakamoto, Shiga|ja|坂本 (滋賀郡)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto, Shiga|qq=}} in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 5 (1720) by the Ōoka family. Originally located on a hill known as Mount Sannō, it was later moved to the grounds of Kumano Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Kumano Ōkami * Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto * Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities * Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto (Hie Shrine deity) == Subordinate Shrines == * {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|qq=}} == Access == * '''By car''': Located along Kumano Shrine Road. Parking available nearby. ** Approximately 12 km south from [[:en:Japan National Route 1|National Route 1 (Japan)]] Amagawa intersection. ** Approximately 2 km north from [[:en:Japan National Route 163|National Route 163 (Japan)]] Kitatawara Bridge intersection. * '''By public transportation''': ** From [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Chigasaki Station|Chigasaki Station]] (North Exit), take a bus bound for [[:en:Bunkyo University|Bunkyo University]] and get off at Takata. Walk for 3 minutes. == Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''': [[:en:January 1|January 1]] * '''Hamaori Festival''': [[:en:July 14|July 14]] * '''festival''': Second Sunday of [[:en:September|September]] == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{cite book|ja|title=Great Japan Geographical Chronicles|volume=Volume 38: New Edition of Sagami Province Topographical Records, Scroll 3|chapter=Ōba Manor, Takata Village, Kumano Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1179219/150}}|publisher=Yuzankaku|date=August 1932}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.com/mem/main/196.html Kanagawa Shrine Map - Kumano Shrine] * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0&PHPSESSID=ff8abecd9e6b1bb6e0394ca1c38c0d2f Kumano Shrine] — [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/ Kanagawa Shrine Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto template}} {{translated page|ja|高田熊野神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|version=98347473|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Kumano Shrines]] [[Category:History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Buildings in Chigasaki]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:%E9%AB%98%E7%94%B0%E7%86%8A%E9%87%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E8%8C%85%E3%83%B6%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]] [[sn:akada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]]

The current shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 2 (1927). Walk for 3 minutes. == Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''': [[:en:January 1|January 1]] * '''Hamaori Festival''': [[:en:July 14|July 14]] * '''festival''': Second Sunday of [[:en:September|September]] == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{cite book|ja|title=Great Japan Geographical Chronicles|volume=Volume 38: New Edition of Sagami Province Topographical Records, Scroll 3|chapter=Ōba Manor, Takata Village, Kumano Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1179219/150}}|publisher=Yuzankaku|date=August 1932}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.com/mem/main/196.html Kanagawa Shrine Map - Kumano Shrine] * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0&PHPSESSID=ff8abecd9e6b1bb6e0394ca1c38c0d2f Kumano Shrine] — [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/ Kanagawa Shrine Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto template}} {{translated page|ja|高田熊野神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|version=98347473|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:%E9%AB%98%E7%94%B0%E7%86%8A%E9%87%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E8%8C%85%E3%83%B6%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]] [[sn:akada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:茅ヶ崎の Municipal History]] [[ja:高田熊野神社 (茅ヶ崎市)]]

{{Shrine | name = Kumano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kumono shrine chigasaki Kanagawa 2023 Jan 20 various 17 42 44 119000.jpeg|270px]]
Torii and Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|55.6|N|139|24|54.6|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[:en:Chigasaki, Kanagawa|Chigasaki]] Takata 1-10-44 | LatitudeDeg = 35 | LatitudeMin = 20 | LatitudeSec = 55.6 | NS = N | LongitudeDeg = 139 | LongitudeMin = 24 | LongitudeSec = 54.6 | EW = E | CountryCode = JP | Map = Japan Kanagawa#Japan | Deities = Kumano Ōkami
Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto
Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities | SacredObject = | Rank = | consecration_year = [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji]] First Year (1658) | MainBuilding = | Alternatenames = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = {{ill|Second Sunday of September|ja|9月第2日曜日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Sunday of September|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Rituals = }} '''Kumano Shrine''' (熊野神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Takata, [[:en:Chigasaki, Kanagawa|Chigasaki]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is historically linked to the family of [[:en:Ōoka Tadasuke|Ōoka Tadasuke]], the famous magistrate of the Edo period. == History == (This section primarily references the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association website{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0 |title=Kumano Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2018-12-27 }}. Additional sources are noted where applicable.) Kumano Shrine was established in [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji]] First Year (1658) by {{ill|Ōoka Tadataka|ja|大岡忠高|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōoka Tadataka|qq=}}, the headman of Takata Village in the [[:en:Kōza District, Kanagawa|Kōza District]], [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]] (father of Ōoka Tadasuke). The shrine enshrines a divided deity from the [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] in [[:en:Muro District, Kii|Muro District, Wakayama]]. The current shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 2 (1927). The subordinate shrine, {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was brought from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] in {{ill|Sakamoto, Shiga|ja|坂本 (滋賀郡)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto, Shiga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 5 (1720) by the Ōoka family. Originally located on a hill known as Mount Sannō, it was later moved to the grounds of Kumano Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Kumano Ōkami * Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto * Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities * Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto (Hie Shrine deity) == Subordinate Shrines == * {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Access == * '''By car''': Located along Kumano Shrine Road. Walk for 3 minutes. == Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''': [[:en:January 1|January 1]] * '''Hamaori Festival''': [[:en:July 14|July 14]] * '''festival''': Second Sunday of [[:en:September|September]] == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{cite book|ja|title=Great Japan Geographical Chronicles|volume=Volume 38: New Edition of Sagami Province Topographical Records, Scroll 3|chapter=Ōba Manor, Takata Village, Kumano Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1179219/150}}|publisher=Yuzankaku|date=August 1932}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.com/mem/main/196.html Kanagawa Shrine Map - Kumano Shrine] * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0&PHPSESSID=ff8abecd9e6b1bb6e0394ca1c38c0d2f Kumano Shrine] — [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/ Kanagawa Shrine Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto template}} {{translated page|ja|高田熊野神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|version=98347473|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:%E9%AB%98%E7%94%B0%E7%86%8A%E9%87%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E8%8C%85%E3%83%B6%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]] [[sn:akada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Chigasaki]] [[ja:高田熊野神社 (茅ヶ崎市)]]

Walk for 3 minutes. == Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''': [[:en:January 1|January 1]] * '''Hamaori Festival''': [[:en:July 14|July 14]] * '''festival''': Second Sunday of [[:en:September|September]] == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{cite book|ja|title=Great Japan Geographical Chronicles|volume=Volume 38: New Edition of Sagami Province Topographical Records, Scroll 3|chapter=Ōba Manor, Takata Village, Kumano Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1179219/150}}|publisher=Yuzankaku|date=August 1932}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.com/mem/main/196.html Kanagawa Shrine Map - Kumano Shrine] * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0&PHPSESSID=ff8abecd9e6b1bb6e0394ca1c38c0d2f Kumano Shrine] — [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/ Kanagawa Shrine Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto template}} {{translated page|ja|高田熊野神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|version=98347473|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:E9%AB%98%E7%94%B0%E7%86%8A%E9%87%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E8%8C%85%E3%83%B6%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]] [[sn:akada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Chigasaki]] [[ja:高田熊野神社 (茅ヶ崎市)]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]]

It is historically linked to the family of [[:en:Ōoka Tadasuke|Ōoka Tadasuke]], the famous magistrate of the Edo period. == History == (This section primarily references the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association website{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0 |title=Kumano Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2018-12-27 }}. Additional sources are noted where applicable.) Kumano Shrine was established in [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji]] First Year (1658) by {{ill|Ōoka Tadataka|ja|大岡忠高|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōoka Tadataka|qq=}}, the headman of Takata Village in the [[:en:Kōza District, Kanagawa|Kōza District]], [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]] (father of Ōoka Tadasuke). The shrine enshrines a divided deity from the [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] in [[:en:Muro District, Kii|Muro District, Wakayama]]. The current shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 2 (1927). The subordinate shrine, {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was brought from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] in {{ill|Sakamoto, Shiga|ja|坂本 (滋賀郡)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto, Shiga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 5 (1720) by the Ōoka family. Originally located on a hill known as Mount Sannō, it was later moved to the grounds of Kumano Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Kumano Ōkami * Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto * Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities * Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto (Hie Shrine deity) == Subordinate Shrines == * {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Access == * '''By car''': Located along Kumano Shrine Road. Additional sources are noted where applicable.) Kumano Shrine was established in [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji]] First Year (1658) by {{ill|Ōoka Tadataka|ja|大岡忠高|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōoka Tadataka|qq=|1=Ōoka Tadataka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7222644}}, the headman of Takata Village in the [[:en:Kōza District, Kanagawa|Kōza District]], [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]] (father of Ōoka Tadasuke). The shrine enshrines a divided deity from the [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] in [[:en:Muro District, Kii|Muro District, Wakayama]]. The current shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 2 (1927). The subordinate shrine, {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was brought from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] in {{ill|Sakamoto, Shiga|ja|坂本 (滋賀郡)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto, Shiga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 5 (1720) by the Ōoka family. Originally located on a hill known as Mount Sannō, it was later moved to the grounds of Kumano Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Kumano Ōkami * Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto * Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities * Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto (Hie Shrine deity) == Subordinate Shrines == * {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Access == * '''By car''': Located along Kumano Shrine Road. Parking available nearby. ** Approximately 12 km south from [[:en:Japan National Route 1|National Route 1 (Japan)]] Amagawa intersection. ** Approximately 2 km north from [[:en:Japan National Route 163|National Route 163 (Japan)]] Kitatawara Bridge intersection. * '''By public transportation''': ** From [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Chigasaki Station|Chigasaki Station]] (North Exit), take a bus bound for [[:en:Bunkyo University|Bunkyo University]] and get off at Takata. Walk for 3 minutes. == Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''': [[:en:January 1|January 1]] * '''Hamaori Festival''': [[:en:July 14|July 14]] * '''festival''': Second Sunday of [[:en:September|September]] == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{cite book|ja|title=Great Japan Geographical Chronicles|volume=Volume 38: New Edition of Sagami Province Topographical Records, Scroll 3|chapter=Ōba Manor, Takata Village, Kumano Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1179219/150}}|publisher=Yuzankaku|date=August 1932}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.com/mem/main/196.html Kanagawa Shrine Map - Kumano Shrine] * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0&PHPSESSID=ff8abecd9e6b1bb6e0394ca1c38c0d2f Kumano Shrine] — [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/ Kanagawa Shrine Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto template}} {{translated page|ja|高田熊野神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|version=98347473|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:E9%AB%98%E7%94%B0%E7%86%8A%E9%87%8E%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E8%8C%85%E3%83%B6%E5%B4%8E%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]] [[sn:akada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Chigasaki]] [[ja:高田熊野神社 (茅ヶ崎市)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Takada Kumano Shrine (Chigasaki)]][[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox religious building|image=File:Kumono shrine chigasaki Kanagawa 2023 Jan 20 various 17 42 44 119000.jpeg|prefecture={{ill|Chigasaki|qid=Q633911}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.34877778|N|139.41516667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11672169]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:30, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:43, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Shrine | name = Kumano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kumono shrine chigasaki Kanagawa 2023 Jan 20 various 17 42 44 119000.jpeg|270px]]
Torii and Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|55.6|N|139|24|54.6|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[:en:Chigasaki, Kanagawa|Chigasaki]] Takata 1-10-44 | LatitudeDeg = 35 | LatitudeMin = 20 | LatitudeSec = 55.6 | NS = N | LongitudeDeg = 139 | LongitudeMin = 24 | LongitudeSec = 54.6 | EW = E | CountryCode = JP | Map = Japan Kanagawa#Japan | Deities = Kumano Ōkami
Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto
Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities | SacredObject = | Rank = | consecration_year = [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji]] First Year (1658) | MainBuilding = | Alternatenames = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = {{ill|Second Sunday of September|ja|9月第2日曜日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Sunday of September|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Rituals = }} '''Kumano Shrine''' (熊野神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Takata, [[:en:Chigasaki, Kanagawa|Chigasaki]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is historically linked to the family of [[:en:Ōoka Tadasuke|Ōoka Tadasuke]], the famous magistrate of the Edo period. == History == (This section primarily references the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association website{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0 |title=Kumano Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2018-12-27 }}. Additional sources are noted where applicable.) Kumano Shrine was established in [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji]] First Year (1658) by {{ill|Ōoka Tadataka|ja|大岡忠高|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōoka Tadataka|qq=|1=Ōoka Tadataka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7222644}}, the headman of Takata Village in the [[:en:Kōza District, Kanagawa|Kōza District]], [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]] (father of Ōoka Tadasuke). The shrine enshrines a divided deity from the [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] in [[:en:Muro District, Kii|Muro District, Wakayama]]. The current shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 2 (1927). The subordinate shrine, {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was brought from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] in {{ill|Sakamoto, Shiga|ja|坂本 (滋賀郡)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto, Shiga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 5 (1720) by the Ōoka family. Originally located on a hill known as Mount Sannō, it was later moved to the grounds of Kumano Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Kumano Ōkami * Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto * Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities * Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto (Hie Shrine deity) == Subordinate Shrines == * {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Access == * '''By car''': Located along Kumano Shrine Road. Parking available nearby. ** Approximately 12 km south from [[:en:Japan National Route 1|National Route 1 (Japan)]] Amagawa intersection. ** Approximately 2 km north from [[:en:Japan National Route 163|National Route 163 (Japan)]] Kitatawara Bridge intersection. * '''By public transportation''': ** From [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Chigasaki Station|Chigasaki Station]] (North Exit), take a bus bound for [[:en:Bunkyo University|Bunkyo University]] and get off at Takata. Walk for 3 minutes. == Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''': [[:en:January 1|January 1]] * '''Hamaori Festival''': [[:en:July 14|July 14]] * '''festival''': Second Sunday of [[:en:September|September]] == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{cite book|ja|title=Great Japan Geographical Chronicles|volume=Volume 38: New Edition of Sagami Province Topographical Records, Scroll 3|chapter=Ōba Manor, Takata Village, Kumano Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1179219/150}}|publisher=Yuzankaku|date=August 1932}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.com/mem/main/196.html Kanagawa Shrine Map - Kumano Shrine] * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0&PHPSESSID=ff8abecd9e6b1bb6e0394ca1c38c0d2f Kumano Shrine] — [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/ Kanagawa Shrine Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto template}} {{translated page|ja|高田熊野神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|version=98347473|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Chigasaki]] [[ja:高田熊野神社 (茅ヶ崎市)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q11672169}}

Torii and Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|55.6|N|139|24|54.6|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[:en:Chigasaki, Kanagawa|Chigasaki]] Takata 1-10-44 | LatitudeDeg = 35 | LatitudeMin = 20 | LatitudeSec = 55.6 | NS = N | LongitudeDeg = 139 | LongitudeMin = 24 | LongitudeSec = 54.6 | EW = E | CountryCode = JP | Map = Japan Kanagawa#Japan | Deities = Kumano Ōkami

Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities | SacredObject = | Rank = | consecration_year = [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji]] First Year (1658) | MainBuilding = | Alternatenames = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = {{ill|Second Sunday of September|ja|9月第2日曜日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Sunday of September|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Rituals = }} '''Kumano Shrine''' (熊野神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Takata, [[:en:Chigasaki, Kanagawa|Chigasaki]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is historically linked to the family of [[:en:Ōoka Tadasuke|Ōoka Tadasuke]], the famous magistrate of the Edo period. == History == (This section primarily references the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association website{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0 |title=Kumano Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2018-12-27 }}. Additional sources are noted where applicable.) Kumano Shrine was established in [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji]] First Year (1658) by {{ill|Ōoka Tadataka|ja|大岡忠高|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōoka Tadataka|qq=|1=Ōoka Tadataka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7222644}}, the headman of Takata Village in the [[:en:Kōza District, Kanagawa|Kōza District]], [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]] (father of Ōoka Tadasuke). The shrine enshrines a divided deity from the [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] in [[:en:Muro District, Kii|Muro District, Wakayama]]. The current shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 2 (1927). The subordinate shrine, {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was brought from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] in {{ill|Sakamoto, Shiga|ja|坂本 (滋賀郡)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto, Shiga|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 5 (1720) by the Ōoka family. Originally located on a hill known as Mount Sannō, it was later moved to the grounds of Kumano Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Kumano Ōkami * Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto * Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities * Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto (Hie Shrine deity) == Subordinate Shrines == * {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Access == * '''By car''': Located along Kumano Shrine Road. Parking available nearby. ** Approximately 12 km south from [[:en:Japan National Route 1|National Route 1 (Japan)]] Amagawa intersection. ** Approximately 2 km north from [[:en:Japan National Route 163|National Route 163 (Japan)]] Kitatawara Bridge intersection. * '''By public transportation''': ** From [[:en:East Japan Railway Company|JR East]] [[:en:Chigasaki Station|Chigasaki Station]] (North Exit), take a bus bound for [[:en:Bunkyo University|Bunkyo University]] and get off at Takata. Walk for 3 minutes. == Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''': [[:en:January 1|January 1]] * '''Hamaori Festival''': [[:en:July 14|July 14]] * '''festival''': Second Sunday of [[:en:September|September]] == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{cite book|ja|title=Great Japan Geographical Chronicles|volume=Volume 38: New Edition of Sagami Province Topographical Records, Scroll 3|chapter=Ōba Manor, Takata Village, Kumano Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1179219/150}}|publisher=Yuzankaku|date=August 1932}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.com/mem/main/196.html Kanagawa Shrine Map - Kumano Shrine] * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0&PHPSESSID=ff8abecd9e6b1bb6e0394ca1c38c0d2f Kumano Shrine] — [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/ Kanagawa Shrine Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto template}} {{translated page|ja|高田熊野神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|version=98347473|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Chigasaki]] [[ja:高田熊野神社 (茅ヶ崎市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11672169}}

It is historically linked to the family of [[:en:Ōoka Tadasuke|Ōoka Tadasuke]], the famous magistrate of the Edo period. == History == (This section primarily references the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association website{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0 |title=Kumano Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2018-12-27 }}. Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities | SacredObject = | Rank = | consecration_year = [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji]] First Year (1658) | MainBuilding = | Alternatenames = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = {{ill|Second Sunday of September|ja|9月第2日曜日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Sunday of September|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Rituals = }} '''Kumano Shrine''' (熊野神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Takata, [[:en:Chigasaki, Kanagawa|Chigasaki]], [[:en:Kanagawa Prefecture|Kanagawa Prefecture]]. It is historically linked to the family of [[:en:Ōoka Tadasuke|Ōoka Tadasuke]], the famous magistrate of the Edo period. == History == (This section primarily references the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association website{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0 |title=Kumano Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2018-12-27 }}. Additional sources are noted where applicable.) Kumano Shrine was established in [[:en:Manji (era)|Manji]] First Year (1658) by {{ill|Ōoka Tadataka|ja|大岡忠高|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōoka Tadataka|1=Ōoka Tadataka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7222644}}, the headman of Takata Village in the [[:en:Kōza District, Kanagawa|Kōza District]], [[:en:Sagami Province|Sagami Province]] (father of Ōoka Tadasuke). The shrine enshrines a divided deity from the [[:en:Kumano Hongū Taisha|Kumano Hongū Taisha]] in [[:en:Muro District, Kii|Muro District, Wakayama]]. The current shrine building was reconstructed in [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 2 (1927). The subordinate shrine, {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was brought from [[:en:Hiyoshi Taisha|Hiyoshi Taisha]] in {{ill|Sakamoto, Shiga|ja|坂本 (滋賀郡)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto, Shiga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Kyōhō|Kyōhō]] 5 (1720) by the Ōoka family. Originally located on a hill known as Mount Sannō, it was later moved to the grounds of Kumano Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Kumano Ōkami * Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto * Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities * Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto (Hie Shrine deity) == Subordinate Shrines == * {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Access == * '''By car''': Located along Kumano Shrine Road. It is historically linked to the family of [[:en:Ōoka Tadasuke|Ōoka Tadasuke]], the famous magistrate of the Edo period. == History == (This section primarily references the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association website{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0 |title=Kumano Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2018-12-27 }}. The subordinate shrine, {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was brought from {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|WD=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha}} in {{ill|Sakamoto, Shiga|ja|坂本 (滋賀郡)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto, Shiga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō}} 5 (1720) by the Ōoka family. Originally located on a hill known as Mount Sannō, it was later moved to the grounds of Kumano Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Kumano Ōkami * Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto * Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities * Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto (Hie Shrine deity) == Subordinate Shrines == * {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Access == * '''By car''': Located along Kumano Shrine Road. Parking available nearby. ** Approximately 12 km south from {{ill|Japan National Route 1|en|Japan National Route 1|WD=Q1191191|lt=National Route 1 (Japan)}} Amagawa intersection. ** Approximately 2 km north from {{ill|Japan National Route 163|en|Japan National Route 163|WD=Q6156843|lt=National Route 163 (Japan)}} Kitatawara Bridge intersection. * '''By public transportation''': ** From {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East}} {{ill|Chigasaki Station|en|Chigasaki Station|WD=Q800622|lt=Chigasaki Station}} (North Exit), take a bus bound for {{ill|Bunkyo University|en|Bunkyo University|WD=Q4997802|lt=Bunkyo University}} and get off at Takata. Walk for 3 minutes. == Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''': {{ill|January 1|en|January 1|WD=Q2150|lt=January 1}} * '''Hamaori Festival''': {{ill|July 14|en|July 14|WD=Q2691|lt=July 14}} * '''festival''': Second Sunday of {{ill|September|en|September|WD=Q123|lt=September}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{cite book|ja|title=Great Japan Geographical Chronicles|volume=Volume 38: New Edition of Sagami Province Topographical Records, Scroll 3|chapter=Ōba Manor, Takata Village, Kumano Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1179219/150}}|publisher=Yuzankaku|date=August 1932}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.com/mem/main/196.html Kanagawa Shrine Map - Kumano Shrine] * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0&PHPSESSID=ff8abecd9e6b1bb6e0394ca1c38c0d2f Kumano Shrine] — [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/ Kanagawa Shrine Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto template}} {{translated page|ja|高田熊野神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|version=98347473|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:高田熊野神社 (茅ヶ崎市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11672169}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Municipal History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Additional sources are noted where applicable.) The current shrine building was reconstructed in {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa}} 2 (1927). Originally located on a hill known as Mount Sannō, it was later moved to the grounds of Kumano Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Kumano Ōkami * Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto * Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities * Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto (Hie Shrine deity) == Subordinate Shrines == * {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Access == * '''By car''': Located along Kumano Shrine Road. [[ja:高田熊野神社 (茅ヶ崎市)]] [[ja:高田熊野神社 (茅ヶ崎市)]] [[ja:高田熊野神社 (茅ヶ崎市)]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Kumono shrine chigasaki Kanagawa 2023 Jan 20 various 17 42 44 119000.jpeg|prefecture={{ill|Chigasaki|WD=Q633911}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.34877778|N|139.41516667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Shrine | name = Kumano Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kumono shrine chigasaki Kanagawa 2023 Jan 20 various 17 42 44 119000.jpeg|270px]]
Torii and Worship Hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=270|coord={{coord|35|20|55.6|N|139|24|54.6|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}} [[:en:Chigasaki, Kanagawa|Chigasaki]] Takata 1-10-44 | LatitudeDeg = 35 | LatitudeMin = 20 | LatitudeSec = 55.6 | NS = N | LongitudeDeg = 139 | LongitudeMin = 24 | LongitudeSec = 54.6 | EW = E | CountryCode = JP | Map = Japan Kanagawa#Japan | Deities = Kumano Ōkami
Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto
Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities | SacredObject = | Rank = | consecration_year = {{ill|Manji (era)|en|Manji (era)|WD=Q1145295|lt=Manji}} First Year (1658) | MainBuilding = | Alternatenames = | Pilgrimage = | AnnualFestival = {{ill|Second Sunday of September|ja|9月第2日曜日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second Sunday of September|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Rituals = }} '''Kumano Shrine''' (熊野神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Takata, [[:en:Chigasaki, Kanagawa|Chigasaki]], {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. It is historically linked to the family of {{ill|Ōoka Tadasuke|en|Ōoka Tadasuke|WD=Q1054143|lt=Ōoka Tadasuke}}, the famous magistrate of the Edo period. == History == (This section primarily references the Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association website{{Cite web|ja|url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0 |title=Kumano Shrine |publisher=Kanagawa Prefecture Shrine Association |accessdate=2018-12-27 }}. Additional sources are noted where applicable.) Kumano Shrine was established in {{ill|Manji (era)|en|Manji (era)|WD=Q1145295|lt=Manji}} First Year (1658) by {{ill|Ōoka Tadataka|ja|大岡忠高|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōoka Tadataka|1=Ōoka Tadataka|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7222644}}, the headman of Takata Village in the {{ill|Kōza District, Kanagawa|en|Kōza District, Kanagawa|WD=Q1202776|lt=Kōza District}}, {{ill|Sagami Province|en|Sagami Province|WD=Q1053732|lt=Sagami Province}} (father of Ōoka Tadasuke). The shrine enshrines a divided deity from the {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|en|Kumano Hongū Taisha|WD=Q705035|lt=Kumano Hongū Taisha}} in {{ill|Muro District, Kii|en|Muro District, Kii|WD=Q6938811|lt=Muro District, Wakayama}}. The current shrine building was reconstructed in {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa}} 2 (1927). The subordinate shrine, {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was brought from {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|WD=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha}} in {{ill|Sakamoto, Shiga|ja|坂本 (滋賀郡)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto, Shiga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō}} 5 (1720) by the Ōoka family. Originally located on a hill known as Mount Sannō, it was later moved to the grounds of Kumano Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Kumano Ōkami * Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto * Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities * Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto (Hie Shrine deity) == Subordinate Shrines == * {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Access == * '''By car''': Located along Kumano Shrine Road. Parking available nearby. ** Approximately 12 km south from {{ill|Japan National Route 1|en|Japan National Route 1|WD=Q1191191|lt=National Route 1 (Japan)}} Amagawa intersection. ** Approximately 2 km north from {{ill|Japan National Route 163|en|Japan National Route 163|WD=Q6156843|lt=National Route 163 (Japan)}} Kitatawara Bridge intersection. * '''By public transportation''': ** From {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East}} {{ill|Chigasaki Station|en|Chigasaki Station|WD=Q800622|lt=Chigasaki Station}} (North Exit), take a bus bound for {{ill|Bunkyo University|en|Bunkyo University|WD=Q4997802|lt=Bunkyo University}} and get off at Takata. Walk for 3 minutes. == Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''': {{ill|January 1|en|January 1|WD=Q2150|lt=January 1}} * '''Hamaori Festival''': {{ill|July 14|en|July 14|WD=Q2691|lt=July 14}} * '''festival''': Second Sunday of {{ill|September|en|September|WD=Q123|lt=September}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{cite book|ja|title=Great Japan Geographical Chronicles|volume=Volume 38: New Edition of Sagami Province Topographical Records, Scroll 3|chapter=Ōba Manor, Takata Village, Kumano Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1179219/150}}|publisher=Yuzankaku|date=August 1932}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.com/mem/main/196.html Kanagawa Shrine Map - Kumano Shrine] * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0&PHPSESSID=ff8abecd9e6b1bb6e0394ca1c38c0d2f Kumano Shrine] — [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/ Kanagawa Shrine Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto template}} {{translated page|ja|高田熊野神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|version=98347473|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:高田熊野神社 (茅ヶ崎市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11672169}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Municipal History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

The shrine enshrines a divided deity from the {{ill|Kumano Hongū Taisha|en|Kumano Hongū Taisha|WD=Q705035|lt=Kumano Hongū Taisha|1=Kumano Hongū Taisha}} in {{ill|Muro District, Kii|en|Muro District, Kii|WD=Q6938811|lt=Muro District, Wakayama|1=Muro District, Kii}}. The current shrine building was reconstructed in {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 2 (1927). The subordinate shrine, {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, was brought from {{ill|Hiyoshi Taisha|en|Hiyoshi Taisha|WD=Q656451|lt=Hiyoshi Taisha|1=Hiyoshi Taisha}} in {{ill|Sakamoto, Shiga|ja|坂本 (滋賀郡)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakamoto, Shiga|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|WD=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō|1=Kyōhō}} 5 (1720) by the Ōoka family. Originally located on a hill known as Mount Sannō, it was later moved to the grounds of Kumano Shrine. == Enshrined Deities == * Kumano Ōkami * Kushimikeno-no-Mikoto * Seven Heavenly Deities and Five Earthly Deities * Ōyamazumi-no-Mikoto (Hie Shrine deity) == Subordinate Shrines == * {{ill|Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja|日枝神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Chigasaki)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Access == * '''By car''': Located along Kumano Shrine Road. Parking available nearby. ** Approximately 12 km south from {{ill|Japan National Route 1|en|Japan National Route 1|WD=Q1191191|lt=National Route 1 (Japan)|1=Japan National Route 1}} Amagawa intersection. ** Approximately 2 km north from {{ill|Japan National Route 163|en|Japan National Route 163|WD=Q6156843|lt=National Route 163 (Japan)|1=Japan National Route 163}} Kitatawara Bridge intersection. * '''By public transportation''': ** From {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Chigasaki Station|en|Chigasaki Station|WD=Q800622|lt=Chigasaki Station|1=Chigasaki Station}} (North Exit), take a bus bound for {{ill|Bunkyo University|en|Bunkyo University|WD=Q4997802|lt=Bunkyo University|1=Bunkyo University}} and get off at Takata. Walk for 3 minutes. == Festivals == * '''New Year's Festival''': {{ill|January 1|en|January 1|WD=Q2150|lt=January 1|1=January 1}} * '''Hamaori Festival''': {{ill|July 14|en|July 14|WD=Q2691|lt=July 14|1=July 14}} * '''festival''': Second Sunday of {{ill|September|en|September|WD=Q123|lt=September|1=September}} == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Reading == * {{cite book|ja|title=Great Japan Geographical Chronicles|volume=Volume 38: New Edition of Sagami Province Topographical Records, Scroll 3|chapter=Ōba Manor, Takata Village, Kumano Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1179219/150}}|publisher=Yuzankaku|date=August 1932}} == External Links == * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.com/mem/main/196.html Kanagawa Shrine Map - Kumano Shrine] * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=667&cd=1206068&scd=&npg=0&PHPSESSID=ff8abecd9e6b1bb6e0394ca1c38c0d2f Kumano Shrine] — [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/ Kanagawa Shrine Association] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto template}} {{translated page|ja|高田熊野神社 (茅ヶ崎市)|version=98347473|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:高田熊野神社 (茅ヶ崎市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11672169}} [[Category:Municipal History of Chigasaki]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
